Recommendation Summary: Establish and approve a system-of-record model for leads, opportunities, customers, invoices, and KPI reporting; remove or control shadow sources; and document end-to-end data lineage.
Standardize source mapping for every critical KPI and reporting object, align cross-system field definitions, and reduce reliance on manual extracts.
Strengthen architecture resilience by formalising lineage, reconciliation rules, and integration ownership across CRM, finance, service, and BI layers.
Enforce stage-based capture rules so required owner, status, date, amount, and next-step fields are completed when work occurs, supported by hygiene controls and accountability.
Tighten capture discipline through validation rules, manager compliance reviews, and role-based training on the fields that drive reporting.
Improve capture consistency at scale using exception alerts, targeted refresher training, and tighter monitoring of high-volume workflows.
Complete a structured data remediation for duplicates, missing values, stale stages, and inconsistent categories, then implement controls that prevent the same issues from reappearing.
Set measurable quality standards for completeness, freshness, classification, and duplicate prevention, with clear stewardship for each record type.
Add proactive quality monitoring for anomalies, master-data drift, and exception trends so reliability holds as transaction volume grows.
Redesign workflow data capture so lifecycle stages, handoffs, timestamps, backlog, and drop-off are visible from lead through invoicing, payment, onboarding, and renewal.
Close workflow blind spots by adding missing handoff fields, aging views, queue controls, and stage-movement reporting across critical processes.
Enhance workflow analytics with stalled-work alerts, cycle-time views, delay analysis, and stage-level risk reporting.
Create an approved KPI dictionary covering exact formulas, inclusion rules, source fields, refresh cadence, owners, and targets for every management metric.
Align reports, dashboards, and teams to one KPI logic set, with defined thresholds and named owners for review and action.
Strengthen the KPI layer with leading indicators, benchmark bands, and clearer action thresholds for management use.
Rebuild the reporting pack around controlled source data, critical management decisions, and exception-based views so leaders are not relying on manual or conflicting reports.
Simplify and standardise dashboards, report cadence, and version control so reporting is timely, consistent, and decision-relevant.
Improve management reporting with role-based layouts, proactive alerts, and clearer views of risk, backlog, and trend movement.
Assign explicit ownership for data quality, KPI governance, dashboard logic, and reporting sign-off, supported by SOPs, review cadence, and correction workflows.
Formalise governance routines, escalation paths, and control checks so standards are enforced consistently across teams.
Elevate governance maturity through control tracking, recurring review of unresolved issues, and structured continuous improvement.
